title: Calonema longicaudum
taxonomy: Calonema longicaudum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, family Orchidaceae, and genus Calonema. It is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. Within the family Orchidaceae, Calonema longicaudum is a type of orchid, which is one of the most diverse plant families in the world. Orchids are further divided into several subfamilies, and Calonema longicaudum specifically falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.

intro: Calonema longicaudum is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Calonema longicaudum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
